evidence: "I loved Mr. Rogers... The story made me tear up slightly..."
"This was a moving story that told of the origins of the paralympic games..."
"The plot was too contrived and I could see the twists coming a mile away."
"The pace of this movie was so slow and so downbeat that I could barely sit through it."
"I was on the edge of my seat from almost beginning to end."
"I think that was to hide the pain he felt. Good job by all."
"The CGI was really outstanding. I kept asking myself how they did it."
"I love Colin Firth and have for years."
"I am a big fan of the West Wing and this is on par, if not better than that."
"The graphics were fantastic, especially for a tv series."
"The story made me tear up slightly and I was taken on a journey of learning to accept..."
"The story made me tear up slightly and I was taken on a journey of learning to accept..."
"I could only feel sad for the loss of Mr. Rogers in the world, a good man with a kind heart."

summary: The user enjoys biographical and feel-good films that evoke strong emotional responses. They appreciate high production quality and are sensitive to pacing, showing a preference for engaging narratives, though they sometimes find plots predictable. Overall, they value storytelling that resonates on a personal level and provides a meaningful experience.
